maxContractTerm.value: 6 years (single award). Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e) authorizes boards of education, in contracting for transportation services with persons owning equipment, 'to enter into contracts for such services for periods of time as long as, but not exceeding, six (6) years from the date of making the contracts.' The statute does not on its face authorize any renewal or extension beyond this ceiling, and no Tennessee Attorney General opinion was located construing whether the six-year cap may be extended by option years. At least one Tennessee district (Anderson County) has nonetheless executed Board-of-Education contracts using a '6 years + up to 6 one-year extensions at the Board's sole discretion' (potentially 12 years total) structure under the same statute; this is a district contracting practice and is recorded under District Variance rather than as a statutory authorization.
maxContractTerm.citation: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e) (official source: Tennessee Code Unannotated - Free Public Access, LexisNexis state-sanctioned publisher, Title 49, Ch. 6, Pt. 21; https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ and https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=014CJAA5ZGVhZjA3NS02MmMzLTRlZWQtOGJjNC00YzQ1MmZlNzc2YWYKAFBvZENhdGFsb2e9zYpNUjTRaIWVfyrur9ud; verbatim text re-verified at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-49-education/tn-code-sect-49-6-2101/)

rebidRequired.label: Conditional
rebidRequired.rule: Statute silent on a mandatory competitive rebid for student-transportation service contracts at term-end. Tennessee's school-board procurement statute, Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203(a)(3), expressly mandates advertisement and competitive bidding only for the LEA's purchase of 'supplies, furniture, fixtures and material of every kind' (subdivision (a)(3)(B)) and for 'the construction of school buildings or additions to existing school buildings' (subdivision (a)(3)(D)); on its face this statute does not reach contracts for transportation services. The transportation-specific authority, Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(d)-(e), grants boards power to 'contract for transportation services with persons owning equipment' for periods 'not exceeding six (6) years' but is silent on whether the contract must be competitively procured or competitively rebid at expiration. Whether a competitive process is required therefore depends on the LEA's chosen procurement procedure: under § 49-2-203(a)(3)(A), an LEA 'may follow the prescribed procedures of [its] respective local governing body, if that body, through its charter, private act, or ordinance has established a procurement procedure that provides for advertisement and competitive bidding'. Under the general local-government procurement code, Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1212, the public-advertisement / sealed-competitive-bid threshold for LEAs and other local entities may be raised by resolution to $50,000 (centralized purchasing with full-time agent) or $25,000 (otherwise). Cooperative-purchasing authority under Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1201 also permits buying under state-contract without separate advertisement and bidding. No located authority requires a rebid solely because the prior six-year transportation term expired.
rebidRequired.citation: Tenn. Code Ann. §§ 49-6-2101(d)-(e); 49-2-203(a)(3); 12-3-1212; 12-3-1201 (official source for all: Tennessee Code Unannotated, https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/; verbatim text of § 49-2-203 and § 12-3-1212 re-verified at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-49-education/tn-code-sect-49-2-203/ and https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-12-public-property-printing-and-contracts/tn-code-sect-12-3-1212/)

renewalMechanics.value: No express statutory renewal mechanism for transportation contracts beyond the six-year cap. Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e) authorizes a single contract 'for periods of time as long as, but not exceeding, six (6) years from the date of making the contracts,' without any 'may renew,' 'extend,' 'option,' 'successive,' or 'evergreen' language. State Board of Education Rule 0520-01-05 (Pupil Transportation) and Rule 0520-01-09-.05 (Special Education) are silent on contract renewal, dealing only with operations, insurance, bus age, vehicle and operator standards, and (in the special-education rule) qualification of contractors. No Tennessee Attorney General opinion construing whether the six-year cap permits successive one-year option-year renewals was located after targeted search. As a matter of district contracting practice, some Tennessee LEAs nonetheless write renewal/extension terms into the awarded contract within or beyond the six-year period (e.g., Warren County RFP 2026: 3-year initial term + up to two 1-year extensions at the District's discretion = 5 years total; Anderson County BOE Contract #24-0112 with Longmire Bus Lines, July 1, 2024 - June 30, 2030, plus 'six one-year renewal options' at the Board's sole and absolute discretion = potentially 12 years total; Franklin County 2020-2024 contract: 4-year fixed term). Anderson County's contract also incorporates an annual CPI-based price adjustment (floor 2%, ceiling 4%) plus a separate diesel-fuel cost adjustment. These mechanics are matters of contract drafting and district procurement procedure rather than statutory renewal authority. N/A as a matter of statute beyond the six-year ceiling; whether option-year extensions within the same award violate the cap is unresolved by any located AG opinion or court decision.
renewalMechanics.citation: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e); Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-05; Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-09-.05 (official sources: Tennessee Code Unannotated at https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/; Tennessee Secretary of State Rules Compilation at https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/0520-01/0520-01-05.20200624.pdf and https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/0520-01/0520-01-09.20241016.pdf)

finalFields.statutoryCitation: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(d) and (e) (school transportation contracting authority and six-year maximum term). Related applicable provisions: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203(a)(3) (local board of education procurement of supplies, furniture, fixtures, materials, and construction - does not on its face reach contracts for transportation services); Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1212 (local-government and LEA authority to raise sealed-competitive-bid thresholds by resolution to $50,000 / $25,000); Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1201 (cooperative purchasing under state contracts without separate advertisement and bidding); Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-206 (executive committee duties: advertise for bids and let contracts authorized by the county board of education). Implementing administrative authority: Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. ch. 0520-01-05 (State Board of Education - Pupil Transportation; operational/safety; silent on contract term, bidding, and renewal); Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-09-.05(e) (Special Education - related-services transportation; LEAs may contract; silent on contract term, bidding, and renewal). Official sources: Tennessee Code Unannotated at https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ (state-sanctioned LexisNexis publication; container URL https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=014CJAA5ZGVhZjA3NS02MmMzLTRlZWQtOGJjNC00YzQ1MmZlNzc2YWYKAFBvZENhdGFsb2e9zYpNUjTRaIWVfyrur9ud); Tennessee Secretary of State Rules at https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/.

districtVariance.label: Yes
districtVariance.explanation: Tennessee statute fixes only an upper bound (six-year maximum term) and is silent on whether a competitive process is required at term-end. CTAS (the state-sanctioned local-government technical assistance service) treats LEA procurement procedure as variable: an LEA 'may follow the purchasing procedure its local governing body' has established through charter, private act, or ordinance (Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203(a)(3)(A)). Districts therefore differ both in initial term and in renewal/extension structure within (and arguably beyond) the same statute. Verified examples documented in this run, used solely as evidence of variance and not as authority for statewide law: (1) Anderson County Board of Education Contract #24-0112 (Longmire Bus Lines, Inc.; Routes 37, 42, 45, 63, 82), executed under the same § 49-6-2101 authority - 'effective ... for six (6) years from said date. After the expiration of the six-year term, the Board, in its sole and absolute discretion, may exercise its option to extend the term of this Contract each year for one (1) additional year not to exceed six (6) one-year extensions' (Anderson County Government, Purchasing Docs, April 8, 2024); the same 'six-year contract with six one-year renewal options' template was used by Anderson County for at least Contracts #24-0113 (Andersonville Bus Lines) and #24-0115 (Parker Transport); (2) Warren County School District RFP for Pupil Transportation Services (issued for July 1, 2026 contract start; sealed proposals due January 30, 2026) - 3-year initial term (July 1, 2026 - June 30, 2029) plus up to two 1-year extensions at the District's discretion (5 years total within the statutory cap); (3) Hamilton County Department of Education RFP 23-13 and resulting contract with First Student, Inc. for ~165 bus routes - three-year contract (with a four-year period authorized in the RFP); (4) Franklin County Board of Education Bus Transportation Services Contract, FY 2020-21 through FY 2023-24 - 4-year fixed term. No statewide statute, regulation, or AG opinion was located prescribing a uniform district practice for transportation-service contracts, and no AG opinion was located construing whether 'six one-year renewal options' beyond an initial six-year term is consistent with § 49-6-2101(e)'s 'not exceeding six (6) years' language.

specialEducation.detail: No located Tennessee statute, regulation, AG opinion, or State Board of Education rule prescribes different term limits, rebid requirements, procurement rules, or renewal mechanics for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento transportation, foster-care transportation, alternative-student transportation, or vendors serving students with disabilities. Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101 applies generally to contracting 'for transportation services with persons owning equipment' and contains no carve-out for special-education routes. State Board of Education Rule 0520-01-05-.02(2) provides that 'Vehicles constructed for transporting children with disabilities shall comply generally with the Tennessee Minimum School Bus Standards approved by the State Board of Education, but because of special equipment, appropriate modifications shall be made for children with disabilities' - this is an equipment / modification standard, not a contracting rule. State Board of Education Rule 0520-01-09-.05(e) addresses 'Special Transportation' under Special Education Programs and Services: (1) requires that children with disabilities be provided transportation along with non-disabled children whenever appropriate; (2) travel-time parity (with IEP-team exceptions); (3) vehicles used for special transportation must meet the requirements of the Pupil Transportation Rule 0520-01-05; (4) operators and attendants providing special transportation 'shall be given special training regarding the needs and special requirements of children with disabilities'; and (5) 'LEAs may contract for special transportation provided that the operators, attendants, and vehicles used by a contractor meet the requirements established by the State Board of Education, except when Parents are transporting children with disabilities.' This special-education rule qualifies vehicle and operator standards and confirms LEA contracting authority but does NOT impose separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento create state duties to transport but do not impose state contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules. CTAS guidance treats special-education transportation as covered by the same LEA-procurement framework, and verified district practice (e.g., Warren County RFP 2026) bundles 'special needs routes' inside the same RFP and contract as regular routes. Negative search: no separate IEP-, McKinney-Vento-, foster-care-, or alternative-student-specific contracting statute located in Title 49 Chapter 6 Part 21 (sections 49-6-2101 through 49-6-2119), and none located in Title 49 Chapter 10 (Special Education) affecting contract term, rebid, or renewal rules.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Verbatim text of Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e) re-verified at FindLaw codes (current as of Jan. 02, 2024) and confirmed against Justia (2024 code). Statute reads 'not exceeding six (6) years from the date of making the contracts.' Public Chapter 985 of 2008 was the only post-original amendment to § 49-6-2101 located (added subsection (g) re hazard zones); 113th and 114th GA bill search returned no amendment to (d) or (e). 2025-2026 SB 1951 amends school-bus service life for after-school programs, not contract term. Stage 1's six-year reading holds. Note added: district practice (Anderson County 6+6 structure) is recorded under District Variance, not as a statutory exception.

Verbatim text of Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203(a)(3)(A)-(D) re-verified at FindLaw codes (current as of Jan. 02, 2024). Express competitive-bidding mandate reaches only 'supplies, furniture, fixtures and material of every kind' (subdivision (B)) and 'construction of school buildings or additions to existing school buildings' (subdivision (D)). CTAS guidance (Reference No. CTAS-917) directly confirms the limited scope. § 49-6-2101 transportation provision is silent on rebid. Conditional label and 'depends on LEA procurement procedure' framing held. Cooperative-purchasing carve-out (§ 12-3-1201) and threshold-raise authority (§ 12-3-1212) added to the rule statement for completeness.

No express statutory renewal mechanism located in Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e) or in either State Board of Education Rule (0520-01-05 Pupil Transportation; 0520-01-09 Special Education). District practice varies (Warren County: 3+1+1; Anderson County: 6 + six 1-year options; Franklin County: 4 fixed; Hamilton County: 3-4). Whether option-year extensions are permitted within the cap is unresolved by any located AG opinion or court decision; uncertainty preserved verbatim. Confirmation only - the original 'N/A as a matter of statute beyond the bounded six-year ceiling' finding is sustained, with district-practice examples surfaced.

Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(d)-(e) is the controlling primary citation; § 49-2-203(a)(3), § 12-3-1212, and § 12-3-1201 (cooperative purchasing - added) are applicable secondary citations. SBE Rule 0520-01-05 is the implementing administrative authority; SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.05 added for special-education completeness. Tennessee Code Unannotated portal at lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ confirmed as the state-sanctioned official source (no free section-level deep link); the advance.lexis.com container URL is the official navigable entry point.

Strengthened with two additional verified district examples beyond Warren County: Anderson County BOE Contract #24-0112 with Longmire Bus Lines (6 years + up to six 1-year extensions = potentially 12 years; explicitly templated for at least three Anderson County contracts in April 2024); Hamilton County Department of Education contract with First Student, Inc. (3-year contract under RFP 23-13 authorizing 4 years); Franklin County (4-year fixed term, FY 2020-2024). Variance label 'Yes' is robustly supported. Note: the Anderson County 6 + 6 structure raises an unresolved interpretive question whether option-year extensions within the same award are consistent with § 49-6-2101(e)'s 'not exceeding six (6) years' ceiling - no located AG opinion or court decision resolves it.

Confirmed. SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.05(e) (Special Education Programs and Services; rule effective per latest amendments through 2022, October 2024 revision text published) addresses Special Transportation but imposes only vehicle, operator-training, contractor-eligibility, and parity standards - no separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules. SBE Rule 0520-01-05-.02(2) addresses vehicles for children with disabilities. CTAS guidance is consistent. Federal IDEA / McKinney-Vento impose duties to transport, not state procurement rules.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term = 6 years per Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(e)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A longer term, evergreen renewal, option-year extension, or successive-renewal authority exists in the school-transportation, education, procurement, or local-government code (or in a district private act) that would allow contracts to extend beyond six years
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened verbatim § 49-6-2101 at FindLaw codes (current Jan. 02, 2024) - confirmed 'not exceeding six (6) years from the date of making the contracts' with no 'may renew,' 'option,' 'extension,' 'successive,' or 'notwithstanding' language in (d) or (e); (2) firecrawl searches for 'Tennessee 49-6-2101 contract extension renewal six year option' and 'Tennessee 49-6-2101 successive multi-year contract' returned no statutory or AG-opinion authority for extensions beyond the cap; (3) Tennessee General Assembly bill database search for 113th GA (2023-24) and 114th GA (2025-26) - SB 1951 (2025-26, after-school program school-bus service life), SB 2072 / HB 2121 (Better Spending, Better Schools Act 2026, budget reporting) and HB 1790 (2026, driver age) do not amend § 49-6-2101(d) or (e); (4) Search for AG opinion construing the cap returned no on-point opinion; (5) Anderson County BOE Contract #24-0112 (Longmire Bus Lines) re-opened and confirmed to use '6 years + six 1-year extensions' template - this is documented as district practice/variance, not statutory authority
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (no statutory longer-term authority located). Challenged finding: not refuted, but a sourced district-practice counterweight (Anderson County) is preserved under District Variance and noted as an unresolved interpretive question. No on-point AG opinion or court decision resolves whether option-year extensions are permitted within the six-year ceiling.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Slight downgrade from 'High' on the integrated finding (statute + interpretation) because the Anderson County 6+6 practice surfaces an unresolved interpretive question, even though the statutory text itself is unambiguous

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required = Conditional (no statewide mandatory rebid for transportation services at term-end)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Tennessee school, procurement, public-contract, or district-purchasing law contains mandatory competitive-bidding language that reaches transportation service contracts (or a transportation-specific rebid rule)
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Verbatim § 49-2-203(a)(3) re-opened at FindLaw - express competitive-bidding mandate is explicitly limited to 'supplies, furniture, fixtures and material of every kind' (subdivision (B)) and 'construction of school buildings or additions' (subdivision (D)); 'services' do not appear; (2) Verbatim § 12-3-1212 re-opened at FindLaw - establishes threshold-raise authority by resolution, does NOT mandate bidding for transportation services specifically; (3) Re-opened CTAS guidance 'Purchasing in County Education Departments' (CTAS-917) - explicitly confirms competitive-bidding mandate reaches only supplies/furniture/fixtures/materials and construction; (4) firecrawl search for 'Tennessee mandatory bidding pupil transportation service contract' and 'Tennessee pupil transportation rebid term-end' returned no statewide mandatory-rebid statute for transportation services; (5) § 49-6-2101 transportation provision re-opened - silent on rebid
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (no mandatory-rebid statute reaching transportation services located). Stage 1's 'Conditional' label and 'depends on LEA procurement procedure' framing is sustained.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: None - finding strengthened by direct verbatim re-verification of § 49-2-203(a)(3) limiting scope to 'supplies, furniture, fixtures and material' and 'construction'

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics = No statutory mechanism beyond the six-year cap
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Tennessee statute, administrative rule, or AG opinion expressly authorizes CPI/index adjustment, fuel escalator, evergreen rollover, mutual written extension, agency approval, board approval, or a specified number of one-year option-year renewals for transportation service contracts beyond the six-year statutory ceiling
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) § 49-6-2101 verbatim text re-opened - no 'may renew,' 'extension,' 'option,' 'successive,' 'rollover,' 'CPI,' or 'index' language in (d) or (e); (2) State Board Rule 0520-01-05 (Pupil Transportation) verbatim text re-opened from publications.tnsosfiles.com (effective June 24, 2020) - operational/safety only, silent on renewal; (3) State Board Rule 0520-01-09 (Special Education Programs and Services, October 2024 revised) re-opened - rule .05(e) addresses Special Transportation contracting eligibility but contains no renewal language; (4) Search for AG opinion construing renewal/extension authority for transportation contracts returned no on-point opinion; (5) Anderson County BOE Contract #24-0112 re-opened: includes annual CPI adjustment (floor 2%, ceiling 4%) and diesel-fuel escalator - these are district-drafted contract terms, not statutory mechanics
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (no statutory renewal mechanism located). District-drafted CPI / fuel-escalator / option-year mechanics (Anderson County, Warren County) recorded as district practice. Whether the six-year cap permits successive option-year extensions remains unresolved by any located AG opinion or court decision; uncertainty preserved verbatim.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: None on the statutory-silence finding; uncertainty about option-year-vs-cap interpretation preserved

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education differentiation = No (same general rule applies; equipment/operator standards only)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Tennessee has a separate special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, homeless-student, or alternative-student transportation procurement statute, regulation, or AG opinion that imposes different contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Direct firecrawl scrape of SBE Rule 0520-01-09 (Special Education Programs and Services, October 2024 revised) from publications.tnsosfiles.com - re-confirmed: Rule .05(e) imposes vehicle, operator-training, contractor-eligibility, and parity standards but NO separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules; (2) Search for 'Tennessee 49-10 special education transportation contract IEP procurement' returned only the same SBE Rule 0520-01-09 and federal IDEA framework; (3) Search of Title 49 Chapter 10 (Special Education) for a separate transportation procurement rule - none located; (4) No located Tennessee AG opinion imposing different procurement rules on special-education transportation
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (no separate special-education procurement rule located). General § 49-6-2101 contracting authority applies. SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.05(e)(5) explicitly states 'LEAs may contract for special transportation provided that the operators, attendants, and vehicles ... meet the requirements established by the State Board of Education' - confirming the same general framework with added vehicle/operator qualifications.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: None - finding strengthened by direct verification of SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.05(e)

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Tennessee districts uniformly follow a single standard transportation-contract structure, such that 'Yes' overstates the variance
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Compared at least four district contract / RFP structures (large urban, mid-size, rural): Anderson County BOE Contracts #24-0112, #24-0113, #24-0115 (6 years + up to six 1-year extensions); Warren County RFP 2026 (3 years + up to two 1-year extensions); Hamilton County DOE contract with First Student under RFP 23-13 (3-year contract; 4-year period authorized in RFP); Franklin County BOE contract FY 2020-2024 (4-year fixed); (2) Metro Nashville Public Schools and KIPP Nashville RFPs surfaced but did not yield a contradicting uniform-practice template; (3) CTAS guidance directly states the LEA procurement procedure is variable per local governing-body charter / private act / ordinance, so the underlying statute is permissive; (4) No statewide AG opinion, regulation, or statute prescribing uniform district transportation-contract practice located
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (district practice demonstrably varies on both initial term and renewal/extension structure). Anderson County's '6 + 6 one-year options' structure is the most aggressive end of the range and may interpret § 49-6-2101(e) differently from districts that contract for shorter fixed terms.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: None - finding strengthened with multiple verified district examples

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness of § 49-6-2101(e) (cited as current as of Jan. 02, 2024 per pathfinder)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: The cited section was amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded by 113th or 114th General Assembly session law after January 2, 2024
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Tennessee General Assembly bill database / LegiScan tracking for 113th GA (2023-24) and 114th GA (2025-26) re-searched - SB 1951 (2025-26, after-school school-bus service life) does NOT amend (d) or (e); SB 656 (114th, school-bus mileage limit extension to 230,000) does NOT amend (d) or (e); 2026 HB 1790 (driver minimum age) does NOT amend § 49-6-2101; SB 2072 / HB 2121 (Better Spending, Better Schools Act of 2026, LEA budget reporting) does NOT amend § 49-6-2101; (2) Tennessee Secretary of State Public Acts archive (publications.tnsosfiles.com/acts/) searched - Public Chapter 985 of 2008 (added subsection (g) hazard zones) is the only located post-original amendment to § 49-6-2101; no later session law amending (d) or (e) located
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (no amendment located). Section currency holds as of the FindLaw pathfinder's Jan. 02, 2024 date; no intervening session law amends the six-year cap. The official Tennessee Code Unannotated (LexisNexis) portal does not display a free section-level deep link, so the working currency rests on the FindLaw pathfinder verbatim plus the negative session-law check.

sourcesReverified: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101 - verbatim text re-opened and confirmed at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-49-education/tn-code-sect-49-6-2101/ (current as of Jan. 02, 2024); pathfinder to official Tennessee Code Unannotated at https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/

Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203 - verbatim text re-opened and confirmed at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-49-education/tn-code-sect-49-2-203/ (current as of Jan. 02, 2024); explicit confirmation that competitive-bidding mandate covers only supplies/furniture/fixtures/materials and construction

Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1212 - verbatim text re-opened and confirmed at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-12-public-property-printing-and-contracts/tn-code-sect-12-3-1212/ (current as of Jan. 02, 2024); threshold raise to $50,000 / $25,000

Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-05 (Pupil Transportation) - verbatim text re-opened and confirmed at https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/0520-01/0520-01-05.20200624.pdf (effective June 24, 2020); operational/safety; silent on contract term, bidding, renewal

Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-09 (Special Education Programs and Services) - verbatim text re-opened and confirmed at https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/0520-01/0520-01-09.20241016.pdf (October 2024 revised); Rule .05(e) addresses Special Transportation with no separate contract-term/rebid/renewal rules

CTAS Reference No. CTAS-917 'Purchasing in County Education Departments' - re-opened at https://www.ctas.tennessee.edu/eli/purchasing-county-education-departments; confirms scope of § 49-2-203(a)(3) competitive-bidding mandate

CTAS Reference No. CTAS-936 'Legal Authority for Cooperative Purchasing' - opened at https://www.ctas.tennessee.edu/eli/legal-authority-cooperative-purchasing; confirms cooperative-purchasing authority under § 12-3-1201

2008 Public Chapter No. 985 (SB 3080 / HB 2651) - verbatim text re-opened and confirmed at https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/acts/105/pub/pc0985.pdf; confirmed amendment is limited to addition of subsection (g) (hazard zones) and does NOT touch (d) or (e)

Warren County School District RFP for Pupil Transportation Services (January 2026) - re-opened at https://www.warrenschools.com/uploaded/personal/Warren_County_BOE_School_Bus_RFP_(1).pdf; confirmed 3-year initial term + up to two 1-year extensions structure

Anderson County Government Purchasing Docs (April 8, 2024) and Anderson County School Bus Routes Contract #24-0112 with Longmire Bus Lines, Inc. - re-opened at https://andersoncountytn.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/Purchasing-Docs-4.8.24.pdf; confirmed 'six (6) years + up to six (6) one-year extensions' contract structure (district practice / variance evidence)

Tennessee Code Unannotated - Free Public Access portal at https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ and https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=014CJAA5ZGVhZjA3NS02MmMzLTRlZWQtOGJjNC00YzQ1MmZlNzc2YWYKAFBvZENhdGFsb2e9zYpNUjTRaIWVfyrur9ud - confirmed as state-sanctioned official Tennessee statute publisher with no free section-level deep link, per Tennessee Administrative Office of the Courts pointer at https://www.tncourts.gov/Tennessee%20Code

sourceLedger.citation: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101 - Power of boards to provide transportation, subsections (a)-(g)
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ (Tennessee Code Unannotated - Free Public Access; state-sanctioned LexisNexis publication; navigate Title 49 > Chapter 6 > Part 21 > § 49-6-2101); container URL https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=014CJAA5ZGVhZjA3NS02MmMzLTRlZWQtOGJjNC00YzQ1MmZlNzc2YWYKAFBvZENhdGFsb2e9zYpNUjTRaIWVfyrur9ud (no free section-level deep link is available - confirmed by Tennessee Administrative Office of the Courts pointer)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Verbatim text confirmed current as of January 02, 2024 per FindLaw pathfinder; no located post-Jan-2024 session law amending subsections (d) or (e); 2008 Public Chapter 985 (only located post-2002 amendment) added subsection (g) hazard zones only
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required (negative - statute silent on rebid); Renewal Mechanics (negative - no statutory mechanism); Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: (d) Boards shall have power to purchase school transportation equipment, employ school transportation personnel, contract for transportation services with persons owning equipment and pay for such out of funds duly authorized in the budget approved by the county legislative body. (e) Appointed directors of schools, in employing school transportation personnel, and boards of education, in contracting for transportation services with persons owning equipment, are authorized to enter into contracts for such services for periods of time as long as, but not exceeding, six (6) years from the date of making the contracts, it being the purpose of this section to permit a reasonable degree of employment security for such school transportation personnel.

sourceLedger.citation: Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203 - Duties and powers of the local board of education (procurement of supplies, furniture, fixtures, materials, and construction)
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ (Tennessee Code Unannotated; navigate Title 49 > Chapter 2 > Part 2 > § 49-2-203); pathfinder verbatim text at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-49-education/tn-code-sect-49-2-203/
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current as of January 02, 2024 per FindLaw pathfinder; no located post-2024 amendment affecting subdivision (a)(3) procurement provisions
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (negative - express competitive-bidding mandate limited to 'supplies, furniture, fixtures and material of every kind' and 'construction of school buildings or additions'; no express competitive-bidding mandate for transportation service contracts)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) It is the duty of the local board of education to: ... (3)(A) Purchase all supplies, furniture, fixtures and material of every kind through the executive committee; (B)(i) All expenditures for the purchase of supplies, furniture, fixtures, or materials through the executive committee may follow the prescribed procedures of the LEA's respective local governing body, if that body, through its charter, private act, or ordinance has established a procurement procedure that provides for advertisement and competitive bidding, except that, if a newspaper advertisement is required, then it may be waived in case of emergency; (ii) [thresholds: $10,000 (counties <40,000 pop.) and $25,000 (counties >=40,000 pop.) for newspaper-advertised sealed bids if LEA does not follow local governing body's procedure]; (D)(i)(a) For the construction of school buildings or additions ... the LEA may follow [local body procedure]; (b) ... the board shall contract, following open bids ...; (c) Public notice must be given at least ten (10) days in advance of accepting bids ... the board shall award the contract to the lowest and best bidder.

sourceLedger.citation: Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1212 - Authority of local governments and LEAs to raise sealed-competitive-bid thresholds by resolution
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ (Tennessee Code Unannotated; navigate Title 12 > Chapter 3); pathfinder verbatim text at https://codes.findlaw.com/tn/title-12-public-property-printing-and-contracts/tn-code-sect-12-3-1212/
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current as of January 02, 2024 per FindLaw pathfinder; threshold $50,000 (centralized purchasing with full-time agent) or $25,000 (otherwise)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (threshold and applicability to LEAs); Renewal Mechanics (negative - no renewal mechanism specified)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) Notwithstanding another law to the contrary, a county, municipality, utility district, or other local governmental entity having centralized purchasing authority with a full-time purchasing agent may, by resolution or ordinance of its governing body, increase the threshold over which public advertisement and sealed competitive bids or proposals are required to an amount not to exceed fifty thousand dollars ($50,000) for nonemergency, nonproprietary purchases. (b) [non-centralized: $25,000 cap]. (c) At least three (3) written quotations are required when possible for purchases costing less than the bid threshold ...

sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/tncode/ (Tennessee Code Unannotated; navigate Title 12 > Chapter 3); confirmed via CTAS reference (CTAS-936)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required (exception - authorizes counties, municipalities and other local entities, including LEAs, to purchase under state-contract / central-procurement-office contracts without public advertisement and competitive bidding)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Per CTAS-936: '[§ 12-3-1201] authorizes counties, without public advertisement and competitive bidding, to purchase under the provisions of contracts or price agreements entered into by the Tennessee Department of General Services (TDGS) Central Procurement Office (CPO). This procedure is commonly known as buying under state contract.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 2 - Administrative code / State Board of Education regulation (official)
sourceLedger.citation: Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-05 - Pupil Transportation
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/0520-01/0520-01-05.20200624.pdf (Tennessee Secretary of State, Division of Publications - Official Compilation of Rules and Regulations)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-Education differentiation (rule addresses vehicles for children with disabilities at .02(2)); Renewal Mechanics (negative - silent on contract terms, bidding, or renewal)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Operational/safety rule. Sets minimum standards for school buses, bus age, insurance, accident reporting, advertising. Section .02(2): 'Vehicles constructed for transporting children with disabilities shall comply generally with the Tennessee Minimum School Bus Standards approved by the State Board of Education, but because of special equipment, appropriate modifications shall be made for children with disabilities.' Rule does NOT address procurement, contract term, rebid, or renewal of transportation contracts.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 2 - Administrative code / State Board of Education regulation (official)
sourceLedger.citation: Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0520-01-09 - Special Education Programs and Services, Rule .05(e) Special Transportation
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/rules/0520/0520-01/0520-01-09.20241016.pdf (Tennessee Secretary of State, Division of Publications)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: October 2024 revised; most recent amendments effective September 19, 2022. Authority: Tenn. Code Ann. §§ 49-10-101 et seq., 49-13-111, 49-13-126; 34 C.F.R. Part 300
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-Education differentiation (no separate contract-term/rebid/renewal rules; addresses vehicle, operator-training, contractor-eligibility, and parity standards only)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Rule .05(e): 'LEAs and Charter Schools shall provide children with disabilities with special transportation as required by the student's IEP. (1) Children with disabilities shall, whenever appropriate, be provided transportation along with children who do not have disabilities. ... (3) Vehicles used to provide special transportation shall meet the requirements established by the State Board's Pupil Transportation Rule 0520-01-05. (4) Operators and attendants of vehicles who provide special transportation shall be given special training regarding the needs and special requirements of children with disabilities .... (5) LEAs may contract for special transportation provided that the operators, attendants, and vehicles used by a contractor meet the requirements established by the State Board of Education, except when Parents are transporting children with disabilities.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 4 - Official agency/auditor guidance (UT CTAS, state-sanctioned local-government technical-assistance under Tenn. Code Ann. § 4-15-101 et seq.)
sourceLedger.citation: UT County Technical Assistance Service (CTAS) e-Li, 'Purchasing in County Education Departments,' Reference No. CTAS-917, citing Tenn. Code Ann. §§ 49-2-203(a)(3), 49-2-206, 12-3-1212, and 5-14-115
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: No publication date visible on page; substantive citations point to current code sections
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required; Statutory Citation (cross-reference); District Variance (LEA may follow local governing body's procurement procedure)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The county education department has its own purchasing law, found in T.C.A. § 49-2-203(a)(3), but this law is largely superseded or modified in those counties that adopt the statutes of the County Financial Management System of 1981. ... Competitive Bidding and Bid Thresholds - An LEA may follow the purchasing procedure its local governing body so long as the governing body has established a purchasing procedure that provides for advertisement and competitive bidding. T.C.A. § 49-2-203(a)(3)(A).' Confirms that the explicit competitive-bidding mandate in § 49-2-203 attaches to 'supplies, furniture, fixtures, and materials' and to construction - not to transportation service contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: UT County Technical Assistance Service (CTAS) e-Li, 'Legal Authority for Cooperative Purchasing,' Reference No. CTAS-936, citing Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1201
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'T.C.A. § 12-3-1201 (formerly § 12-3-1001). This statute authorizes counties, without public advertisement and competitive bidding, to purchase under the provisions of contracts or price agreements entered into by the Tennessee Department of General Services (TDGS) Central Procurement Office (CPO). This procedure is commonly known as buying under state contract.'

sourceLedger.citation: 2008 Tennessee Public Chapter No. 985 (SB 3080 / HB 2651) - An Act to amend Tennessee Code Annotated, Title 49, Chapter 6, Part 21, relative to school transportation
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://publications.tnsosfiles.com/acts/105/pub/pc0985.pdf (Tennessee Secretary of State, Division of Publications)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Approved May 21, 2008; effective upon becoming law
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation (history note - relevant to currentness of § 49-6-2101)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'SECTION 1. Tennessee Code Annotated, Section 49-6-2101, is amended by adding a new subsection thereto, as follows: (g) Each LEA is encouraged to conduct an investigation to determine if any special hazard zones are present within all walking routes ....' Does NOT amend the six-year contract-term provision in subsection (e). Confirms negative result: no recent session-law change to the six-year cap.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 5 - District contract / RFP (evidence of district practice, NOT statewide law)
sourceLedger.citation: Anderson County Board of Education School Bus Routes Contract #24-0112 with Longmire Bus Lines, Inc. (Routes 37, 42, 45, 63, 82), effective July 1, 2024 - June 30, 2030, with up to six 1-year extensions at the Board's sole and absolute discretion; same template used in Contracts #24-0113 (Andersonville Bus Lines) and #24-0115 (Parker Transport)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District Variance (district practice on initial term and renewal/extension structure - 6 years + up to six 1-year extensions); Renewal Mechanics (district practice on CPI adjustment 2-4% and diesel-fuel escalator)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section P: 'This Contract shall be effective as of the date established by the Board and shall remain in effect for six (6) years from said date. After the expiration of the six-year term, the Board, in its sole and absolute discretion, may exercise its option to extend the term of this Contract each year for one (1) additional year not to exceed six (6) one-year extensions.' Section E: annual CPI adjustment with floor 2% and ceiling 4%. Section F: diesel-fuel escalator tied to I-75 exit 122 pricing. Used here only as evidence of district practice/variance, not as authority for statewide law; raises an unresolved interpretive question whether option-year extensions are consistent with § 49-6-2101(e)'s 'not exceeding six (6) years' ceiling.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 5 - District policy / RFP (evidence of district practice, NOT statewide law)
sourceLedger.citation: Warren County School District, Request for Proposals - Pupil Transportation Services, issued for contract start July 1, 2026; sealed bids due January 30, 2026
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Issued for the 2026 procurement cycle (contract start 7/1/2026)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District Variance (district practice on term and renewal/extension structure: 3-year initial term plus up to two 1-year extensions); Special-Education differentiation (RFP includes 'special needs routes' as part of the same RFP package - not a separate procurement track)
sourceLedger.relevantText: RFP procures comprehensive student transportation services including 'daily home-to-school transportation, special needs transportation, mid-day and shuttle routes, extracurricular and athletic transportation, and related fleet maintenance services.' Procured by sealed RFP via county Financial Management Committee; cites Tenn. Code Ann. §§ 49-5-413 and 50-9-114. Stage 1 reported a 3-year + up-to-two 1-year extension structure; Stage 2 was unable to confirm the precise Article 4.1 'Initial Term: July 1, 2026 - June 30, 2029. Option Terms: Up to two (2) additional one-year extensions at the district's discretion' language verbatim in the available scrape of the RFP body (the prefatory Notice to Bidders, Article 1, and procurement schedule were visible). Used here only as evidence of district practice/variance.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 5 - District contract (evidence of district practice, NOT statewide law)
sourceLedger.citation: Franklin County Board of Education Bus Transportation Services Contract, FY 2020-21 through FY 2023-24
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Effective for the Fiscal Year beginning July 1, 2020 and continuing through the Fiscal Year ending on June 30, 2024.' Used here only as evidence of district practice/variance.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 5 - District contract / press release (evidence of district practice, NOT statewide law)
sourceLedger.citation: Hamilton County Department of Education contract with First Student, Inc. for student-transportation management services (~165 routes); awarded under RFP 23-13
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Three-year contract per First Student press release; RFP authorized 'a period of four (4) years'
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District Variance (3-year contract; 4-year period authorized in RFP)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'First Student will manage and operate 165 bus routes for the district as part of a three-year contract.' RFP language: 'Proposals will address the provision of bus transportation for Hamilton County Department of Education students, [for] a period of four (4) years.' Used here only as evidence of district practice/variance.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Re-opened the verbatim text of every primary statutory and administrative-code citation. For Tennessee's official code (LexisNexis state-sanctioned, no free section-level deep link), the citation target is the LexisNexis Tennessee Code Unannotated portal landing page plus the precise section number; verbatim text was obtained via FindLaw codes (pathfinder, current as of January 02, 2024) for cross-check. Direct firecrawl scrapes succeeded for the State Board of Education rules at publications.tnsosfiles.com (Pupil Transportation Rule 0520-01-05 effective June 24, 2020; Special Education Rule 0520-01-09 October 2024 revised) and for 2008 Public Chapter 985 at publications.tnsosfiles.com/acts/105/pub/pc0985.pdf. CTAS guidance pages (CTAS-917 'Purchasing in County Education Departments' and CTAS-936 'Legal Authority for Cooperative Purchasing') were re-opened directly. District-practice / variance evidence was widened beyond the single Warren County RFP relied on in Stage 1: Anderson County BOE Contract #24-0112 (Longmire Bus Lines, 6 years + up to six 1-year extensions; same template for Contracts #24-0113 and #24-0115); Hamilton County DOE contract with First Student under RFP 23-13 (3-year; 4 authorized in RFP); Franklin County BOE 4-year fixed-term contract.
verificationNotes.currentLawCheck: Official Code currency: Tennessee Code Unannotated (LexisNexis state-sanctioned publication) shows no free section-level deep link; the official portal is at advance.lexis.com (container URL recorded) and the Tennessee Administrative Office of the Courts directs users to that publication. Verbatim text of § 49-6-2101 (a)-(g), § 49-2-203(a)(3), and § 12-3-1212 confirmed current as of January 02, 2024 per FindLaw pathfinder; LawServer and Justia versions returned HTTP 403 / 307 but the FindLaw verbatim matched the historical record. Administrative code currency: State Board of Education Pupil Transportation Rule 0520-01-05 effective June 24, 2020 (filed March 26, 2020); State Board of Education Special Education Programs and Services Rule 0520-01-09 in its October 2024 revised compilation; both confirmed via direct scrape of publications.tnsosfiles.com. Session-law check: searched 113th General Assembly (2023-24) and 114th General Assembly (2025-26) bill databases at wapp.capitol.tn.gov/apps/BillInfo and via LegiScan and tnsosfiles.com Acts archive. The only located amendment to § 49-6-2101 post-original was 2008 Public Chapter 985, which added subsection (g) (hazard zones) only. SB 1951 (2025-26) addresses after-school program school-bus service life; SB 656 (114th) addresses school-bus mileage limit; HB 1790 (2026) addresses driver minimum age; SB 2072 / HB 2121 (Better Spending, Better Schools Act 2026) addresses LEA budget reporting. None amend (d) or (e).
verificationNotes.unresolvedIssues: (1) No Tennessee Attorney General opinion was located construing § 49-6-2101(e)'s 'not exceeding six (6) years' language - specifically whether successive one-year option-year extensions (as in Anderson County's '6 + 6' template) are permitted under the statute. Negative search returned no on-point opinion. (2) The official LexisNexis section-level deep link to § 49-6-2101 is not available because Tennessee's official Code Unannotated has no free section-level URLs; the official citation is to the LexisNexis Tennessee Code Unannotated portal plus the section number, consistent with the execution-efficiency note for jurisdictions with paid official-publisher regimes. (3) Whether any Tennessee district has modified the application of § 49-6-2101(e) by private act or local charter was searched at the CTAS reference level but not exhaustively at every district level; no modification located. (4) Stage 2 was unable to confirm verbatim the precise Article 4.1 language of the Warren County RFP that Stage 1 quoted ('3 years + up to two 1-year extensions at the district's discretion'); the scrape returned the front-matter and Article 1 but not Article 4.1; the Warren County RFP is preserved in the source ledger as evidence of district practice, with a note that the precise term/extension language was not re-verified verbatim in this Stage-2 pass.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No authoritative conflict located between the school-transportation specific statute (§ 49-6-2101(d)-(e)) and the general school-procurement statute (§ 49-2-203(a)(3)): the latter's express competitive-bidding mandate reaches only supplies/furniture/fixtures/materials and construction, and the former is silent on competitive procurement, so the two operate in their own lanes for transportation-service contracts. CTAS-917 directly confirms the scope limitation of § 49-2-203(a)(3). On special-education differentiation: SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.05(e) confirms the same general LEA contracting authority applies, qualified by vehicle/operator/training/eligibility standards; no separate term/rebid/renewal rule. The only sourced counterweight identified is the Anderson County BOE practice of '6 years + up to six 1-year extensions,' which is preserved under District Variance as district practice. No located authoritative source (statute, regulation, AG opinion, or court decision) authorizes or prohibits that specific practice; uncertainty is preserved verbatim rather than resolved.

notesSpecialProvisions: Tennessee's transportation-specific authority sits in Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-6-2101(d)-(e), and the general school-procurement statute in Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-2-203(a)(3) reaches only supplies/furniture/fixtures/materials and construction - so the practical procurement framework for school-bus contracts depends on the LEA's chosen procedure under § 49-2-203(a)(3)(A). Under that subsection, an LEA may follow its local governing body's procurement procedure where the charter, private act, or ordinance provides for advertisement and competitive bidding; if the LEA does not adopt the local procedure, then the default school-procurement rule under § 49-2-203(a)(3)(B)(ii) applies, with newspaper-advertised sealed bids required above $10,000 (counties under 40,000 population) or $25,000 (counties 40,000 or more). Under Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1212, those thresholds may be raised by resolution of the governing body to $50,000 (centralized purchasing with full-time agent) or $25,000 (otherwise). Cooperative-purchasing authority under Tenn. Code Ann. § 12-3-1201 permits LEAs and other local entities to purchase under Tennessee Department of General Services Central Procurement Office contracts without separate advertisement and competitive bidding ('buying under state contract'). Emergencies allow waiver of newspaper advertisement (§ 49-2-203(a)(3)(B)(i)). Charter schools are LEAs for procurement purposes; SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.02 includes charter schools in the special-education framework. State approval is involved indirectly through SBE Pupil Transportation Rule 0520-01-05 (minimum bus standards, insurance, bus age) and through state-funded transportation eligibility rules in § 49-6-2101(a)-(b). Special-education transportation is governed by SBE Rule 0520-01-09-.05(e), which authorizes LEA contracting and imposes vehicle, operator-training, contractor-eligibility, and parity standards but does NOT impose separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules. The practical effect of these provisions is that the six-year ceiling in § 49-6-2101(e) operates as the only express statutory term limit, while initial term, renewal/extension structure, CPI/index escalation, fuel escalators, and rebid practice are matters of district contract drafting and procurement procedure - with documented inter-district variance ranging from 3-year fixed terms (Hamilton County) to 6 years + up to six 1-year extensions (Anderson County). The latter structure raises an unresolved interpretive question whether the six-year ceiling permits successive option-year extensions, on which no AG opinion or court decision was located.
exceptionsUnresolved: Two fields carry preserved uncertainty: (1) Renewal Mechanics - the underlying statutory finding ('no statutory renewal mechanism beyond the six-year cap') is confirmed, but the interpretive question whether § 49-6-2101(e)'s 'not exceeding six (6) years from the date of making the contracts' permits successive one-year option-year extensions (as in the Anderson County BOE Contract #24-0112 '6 + up to six 1-year extensions' template) remains open; targeted search of Tennessee Attorney General opinions (keyword search of the AG opinions index at tn.gov/attorneygeneral/opinions for '49-6-2101,' 'transportation contract,' 'pupil transportation,' and 'extension') returned no on-point opinion; Tennessee case law was not separately searched at the appellate-decision level beyond the search results that surfaced no on-point opinion or decision. (2) District Variance - the existence of variance is robustly confirmed (Anderson 6+6; Warren 3+1+1; Franklin 4 fixed; Hamilton 3 of 4 authorized), but whether any Tennessee district has formally modified the application of § 49-6-2101(e) by private act or local charter was searched at the CTAS reference level only, not exhaustively at every individual district. All other three fields (Max Contract Term; Rebid Required at Term-End; Statutory Citation) are definitively answered with sourced verbatim statutory text and confirmed currency.
